payload.title: score.py needs pre-built release trust binary; no auto-bootstrap
payload.description:
score.py originally invoked 'cargo run -q --release -p trust -- check ...'. Since the trust crate dogfoods named-arg syntax (RT-31), the build needs RUSTC_WRAPPER=trust-rustc to compile. score.py doesn't set RUSTC_WRAPPER, so the build silently failed → every trust-condition file scored caught=✗ → fake 0/15 catch rate (initial run of 005 hit this).

Fix applied: changed score.py to invoke target/release/trust directly. New requirement: a release binary must exist beforehand, built via 'RUSTC_WRAPPER=$(pwd)/target/debug/trust-rustc cargo build --release -p trust' (or similar).

Follow-up: either (a) make score.py bootstrap that build automatically, (b) ship a shell wrapper that score.py calls, or (c) check the binary's presence and fail loudly with a helpful 'run X first' message rather than silently scoring 0.
